title: Warm Smoked German Sauerbraten Casserole
description: A hearty, comforting German dish with a winey flavor profile and gooey cheese pulling from the crispy top, inspired by the classic Sauerbraten recipe.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, brown the beef cubes in butter until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes, then set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes, then add the caraway seeds and cook for another minute.
Add the wine, broth, bay leaves, and browned beef back into the pot, cover, and bring to a simmer.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the beef for about 2-1/2 hours, or until tender, then strain the sauce and set aside.
Shred the beef with two forks and mix with the sauce, then stir in the pul biber and smoked paprika.
In a separate bowl, mix the shredded cheddar cheese with the crushed rye bread and a pinch of salt and pepper.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, arrange the beef mixture in an even layer, then sprinkle the cheese mixture on top, finishing with a layer of remaining beef sauce.
Drizzle the top with the beaten eggs for a golden-brown crust and dot with butter.
Bake in the preheated oven until the top is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly, about 25-30 minutes.
Let it rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Slice and serve hot, garnished with additional caraway seeds and chopped fresh parsley if desired.
